artifact_identity.producer: scripts/build_router.py from sibling generated catalogs, routing schemas, and bounded route mechanics
artifact_identity.consumer_expectation: Consumers verify artifact_identity, router_version, schema conformance, build_router --check, validate_router, and source-owned next-hop refs before using routing surfaces for dispatch.
artifact_identity.privacy_boundary: public route refs and compact summaries only; no private source payloads, secrets, live runtime state, or owner-corpus copies
artifact_identity.content_identity: generated routing family rooted at generated/aoa_router.min.json and rebuilt by scripts/build_router.py

entries.name: plan-diff-apply-verify-report
entries.summary: Safe workflow for agent-driven changes using explicit planning, scoped diffs, explicit validation, and concise reporting.

entries.name: source-of-truth-layout
entries.summary: Repository document role separation pattern that keeps status, plans, history, decisions, and run instructions in distinct canonical homes.

entries.name: contract-first-smoke-summary
entries.summary: Runnable smoke pattern where each smoke path emits a machine-readable summary that becomes the primary validation contract.

entries.name: intent-plan-dry-run-contract-chain
entries.summary: Safe workflow that normalizes intent into a traceable plan, validates it with dry-run, and enforces contract checks before any real execution path exists.

entries.name: new-intent-rollout-checklist
entries.summary: Checklist for safely adding a new intent type to an intent-plan-dry-run chain without contract drift.

entries.name: latest-alias-plus-history-copy
entries.summary: Dual-write summary pattern that keeps a stable latest alias, preserves nested history, and prevents double-count accumulation.

entries.name: signal-first-gate-promotion
entries.summary: Staged pattern for promoting an observed validation signal into strict enforcement without losing diagnostics or widening the fail surface too early.

entries.name: published-summary-remediation-snapshot
entries.summary: Read-only aggregation pattern that turns latest published summaries into a bounded remediation snapshot without replaying history or changing runtime behavior.

entries.name: lightweight-status-snapshot
entries.summary: Keep top-level status documents short and link-driven, while routing detailed execution state and history to their canonical homes.

entries.name: telemetry-integrity-snapshot
entries.summary: Read-only integrity verdict that checks telemetry and summary-layout invariants across latest published summaries without widening the hard fail surface.

entries.name: required-vs-optional-source-rendering
entries.summary: Distinguish strict required sources from tolerant optional sources so operator-facing summary surfaces remain useful without hiding true hard failures.

entries.name: deterministic-context-composition
entries.summary: Compose agent context from smaller fragments into a stable generated artifact with deterministic ordering and source traceability.

entries.name: single-source-rule-distribution
entries.summary: Keep one canonical rule source and distribute it to multiple agent-facing instruction surfaces without turning each target into a hand-maintained source of truth.

entries.name: tdd-slice
entries.summary: Implement a bounded behavior slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor limits.

entries.name: contract-test-design
entries.summary: Make a boundary explicit by defining expected inputs, outputs, and verification around the contract rather than around hidden internals.

entries.name: bounded-context-map
entries.summary: Reduce semantic drift by naming bounded contexts, separating responsibilities, and making handoff interfaces visible for docs and scoping work.

entries.name: property-invariants
entries.summary: Express stable system or domain truths as invariant-oriented tests or checks so broad behavior is constrained beyond a small handpicked example set.

entries.name: markdown-technique-section-lift
entries.summary: Lift stable technique markdown sections into derived section-level units while keeping the bundle markdown authoritative.

entries.name: frontmatter-metadata-spine
entries.summary: Treat bounded frontmatter and derived catalog outputs as a metadata spine for bundle routing without replacing markdown meaning or growing schema past current needs.

entries.name: evidence-note-provenance-lift
entries.summary: Use typed evidence note kinds and note paths as bounded provenance handles in derived manifests without flattening them into a note graph.

entries.name: bounded-relation-lift-for-kag
entries.summary: Lift small typed direct relations into bounded edge hints for derived consumers without turning them into graph semantics.

entries.name: risk-and-negative-effect-lift
entries.summary: Lift richer `Risks` language into bounded caution-oriented lookup and reuse without turning caution into metadata, scoring, or generated policy.

entries.name: stateless-single-shot-agent
entries.summary: Keep shell-side agent work mostly stateless and bounded to one confirmed step per invocation so runs stay composable, reviewable, and low-memory by default.

entries.name: upstream-mirroring-with-provenance
entries.summary: Mirror upstream-owned content into a curated local collection through an explicit source manifest and preserved provenance so the local copy stays reviewable without pretending to be the canonical source.

entries.name: capability-spec-versioning
entries.summary: Keep agent-facing capability contracts in a versioned, reviewable spec so capability changes stay explicit and reusable without turning the spec into routing or registry policy.

entries.name: session-capture-as-repo-artifact
entries.summary: Capture AI coding sessions as versioned repo artifacts so project history stays searchable, reviewable, and reusable without turning session logs into memory or instruction policy.

entries.name: cross-agent-skill-propagation
entries.summary: Keep one canonical skill or rule source and propagate it to multiple agent-facing targets without turning each target into a hand-maintained source of truth.

entries.name: confirmation-gated-mutating-action
entries.summary: Require one explicit confirmation seam before a read or plan flow crosses into a mutating action so the action stays reviewable without widening into a multi-step autonomous loop.

entries.name: nested-rule-loading
entries.summary: Load hierarchical rule layers with explicit precedence so nested additions stay subordinate to one canonical source of ownership.

entries.name: fragmented-agent-context
entries.summary: Keep agent context in bounded fragments before deterministic assembly so modular authoring stays reviewable without collapsing into the final generated artifact.

entries.name: shell-composable-agent-invocation
entries.summary: Make agent runs composable as shell-side one-shot tools through explicit stdin, stdout, files, and pipes without widening into generic shell advice or autonomous loops.

entries.name: context-report-for-ci
entries.summary: Emit CI-facing reports for context composition, source coverage, token-estimate drift, and related composition checks without turning the report surface into the composition technique itself.

entries.name: decision-rationale-recording
entries.summary: Keep one meaningful decision in a reviewable note with context, options, rationale, and consequences while staying out of source-of-truth governance and architecture taxonomy.

entries.name: public-safe-artifact-sanitization
entries.summary: Turn sensitive technical material into a shareable artifact by removing, redacting, or generalizing details while preserving the lesson and staying distinct from approval gating or execution planning.

entries.name: profile-preset-composition
entries.summary: Compose small reusable profiles into named presets so runtime posture stays reviewable without flattening composition into one opaque config or launcher doctrine.

entries.name: render-truth-before-startup
entries.summary: Render the actual composed runtime truth before startup so operators review the effective service and config view instead of relying only on declared profiles.

entries.name: contextual-host-doctor
entries.summary: Run selector-aware host-readiness checks before startup so environment drift becomes visible for the chosen runtime without widening into generic monitoring or lifecycle control.

entries.name: one-command-service-lifecycle
entries.summary: Start and stop a bounded local service stack through one explicit lifecycle entrypoint so prerequisite checks, visible runtime status, and clean shutdown stay reviewable without widening into generic launcher or platform doctrine.

entries.name: baseline-first-additive-profile-benchmarks
entries.summary: Benchmark one stable baseline profile first, then compare additive profiles against the same measurement surface and artifact shape so richer profiles stay additive and off the default path.

entries.name: skill-vs-command-boundary
entries.summary: Separate reusable skill meaning from user-facing command invocation so shared capability stays portable without collapsing into slash-command syntax or command-specific workflow policy.

entries.name: skill-marketplace-curation
entries.summary: Curate a local discoverability layer over upstream-owned skill sources so selection stays editorial and reviewable without pretending the catalog owns sync, capability meaning, or registry policy.

entries.name: upstream-skill-health-checking
entries.summary: Check upstream-owned skill sources for availability and manifest-readiness before surfacing them as selectable inputs so broken entries stay visible and reviewable without widening into generic monitoring, registry governance, or security doctrine.

entries.name: multi-source-primary-input-provenance
entries.summary: Mark primary versus supporting source inputs explicitly when bridging multiple source surfaces so downstream readers and synthesis keep provenance priority visible without turning the bridge into graph semantics or ranking doctrine.

entries.name: versionable-session-transcripts
entries.summary: Package already-saved AI session transcripts as readable, versionable Markdown artifacts so review, handoff, and selective sharing stay possible without reopening capture semantics or turning transcript history into memory or instruction authority.

entries.name: witness-trace-as-reviewable-artifact
entries.summary: Preserve a bounded witness trace as a reviewable artifact with step visibility, state-delta notes, and human-readable summary so a nontrivial run can be inspected before any writeback or promotion without creating a new memory-object kind.

entries.name: repo-doc-surface-lift
entries.summary: Lift one bounded set of authoritative repo docs and status files into derived routing knowledge without replacing the authored docs or widening into a docs taxonomy.

entries.name: github-review-template-lift
entries.summary: Lift authored GitHub issue and pull-request review templates into derived intake knowledge without turning templates into workflow automation or policy scoring.

entries.name: semantic-review-surface-lift
entries.summary: Lift authored semantic-review docs into derived boundary-review knowledge without creating automatic semantic verdicts.

entries.name: dependency-aware-task-graph
entries.summary: Model multi-step coding work as explicit dependency nodes and edges so blocked state and ready work stay reviewable instead of hiding in chat memory.

entries.name: ready-work-from-blocker-graph
entries.summary: Derive the next bounded work queue from blocker-free graph state so operators choose from what is truly ready instead of narrating readiness from memory.

entries.name: commit-triggered-background-review
entries.summary: Trigger a bounded background review after a commit so findings survive as inspectable artifacts without widening into autonomous merge, rewrite, or CI governance.

entries.name: review-findings-compaction
entries.summary: Compact and revalidate review findings against current code so repeated or stale findings do not overwhelm the current review surface.

entries.name: local-first-session-index
entries.summary: Build a local searchable index over already-saved session artifacts so teams can browse or query saved history without reopening capture semantics or turning the index into memory or dashboard doctrine.

entries.name: compaction-resilient-skill-loading
entries.summary: Re-source-route skill availability after context compaction so agents can reload needed skills from canonical sources without widening into full context reconstruction or prompt stuffing.

entries.name: requirements-design-tasks-ladder
entries.summary: Separate requirements, design, and task slices so implementation planning stays reviewable without importing a full spec-driven methodology stack.

entries.name: channelized-agent-mailbox
entries.summary: Keep agent communication inside durable named channels with ordered replay and explicit acknowledgment so coordination survives session gaps without widening into a full messaging platform or handoff-governance stack.

entries.name: structured-handoff-before-compaction
entries.summary: Write one structured handoff artifact before compaction or session rollover so the next session can resume from explicit state instead of hidden memory or transcript replay.

entries.name: receipt-confirmed-handoff-packet
entries.summary: Require an explicit receipt state for a handoff packet before the receiving side continues so ownership transfer stays reviewable instead of being inferred from delivery or silence.

entries.name: git-verified-handoff-claims
entries.summary: Verify concrete handoff claims against visible git state before continuation so the next session trusts repo evidence rather than memory or summary prose alone.

entries.name: session-opening-ritual-before-work
entries.summary: Start a resumed or handed-off session with one visible read-and-verify ritual before the first mutation so work begins from current state rather than stale assumptions.

entries.name: cross-repo-resource-map-bootstrap
entries.summary: Bootstrap cross-repo work from one explicit resource map so the next session can see which repos and surfaces matter before deeper continuation begins.

entries.name: episode-bounded-agent-loop
entries.summary: Break longer work into explicit episodes with checkpoints and continue, stop, or escalate decisions so continuation stays reviewable instead of slipping into open-ended autonomy.

entries.name: versioned-agent-registry-contract
entries.summary: Keep registry-facing capability entries reviewable as named versioned records with explicit references and metadata so publication stays bounded without widening into discovery policy or registry product doctrine.

entries.name: capability-discovery
entries.summary: Keep capability lookup reviewable as explicit bounded queries over published registry entries so discovery stays separate from ranking, marketplace curation, trust policy, and registry product doctrine.

entries.name: mcp-gateway-proxy
entries.summary: Front multiple configured MCP servers through one bounded gateway proxy so callers use one reviewable tool surface with explicit metadata and sanitization instead of binding directly to each upstream server.

entries.name: transcript-replay-artifact
entries.summary: Turn already-saved session history into a replayable artifact so reviewers can inspect message flow and timeline without reopening capture semantics or widening into hosted replay-platform doctrine.

entries.name: transcript-linked-code-lineage
entries.summary: Link code history back to saved session evidence so reviewers can reopen the originating transcript or rationale without widening the bundle into generic repo analytics or memory doctrine.

entries.name: fail-closed-evidence-gate
entries.summary: Stop mutating execution at the boundary unless an explicit allow verdict exists, and emit reviewable evidence for blocked or allowed paths instead of relying on best-effort warnings.

entries.name: approval-bound-durable-jobs
entries.summary: Keep longer-running work durable across one explicit approval seam so checkpoint, pause, and resume remain reviewable without widening into a scheduler or orchestration platform.

entries.name: two-stage-document-ocr-pipeline
entries.summary: Stage OCR as detect or layout -> recognize -> structured handoff so downstream extraction stays reviewable, interchangeable, and confidence-aware instead of collapsing OCR and field logic into one opaque step.

entries.name: template-backed-field-extraction-after-ocr
entries.summary: Extract bounded fields after OCR through explicit templates, heuristics, and missing-or-conflict signaling so structured receipt-like data stays reviewable instead of being guessed by one opaque parser.

entries.name: perceptual-media-dedupe-with-threshold-review
entries.summary: Group near-duplicate media through perceptual similarity and thresholded review buckets so cleanup stays reviewable instead of collapsing into silent deletion or one-threshold dogma.

entries.name: semantic-media-bucketing-with-vision-plus-ocr
entries.summary: Bucket mixed media through bounded visual semantics plus OCR side text so screenshots, memes, receipts, and other media classes remain reviewable under explicit confidence gates instead of widening into open-ended multimodal automation.

entries.name: telegram-export-normalization-to-local-store
entries.summary: Normalize Telegram messages and media into a resumable local store with visible provenance so later workflows can inspect, route, or distill the data without collapsing auth, session, or memory doctrine into the storage contract.

entries.name: session-donor-harvest
entries.summary: Distill a reviewed session artifact into a bounded donor pack of reusable units so candidate practice, workflow, and scenario objects can be evaluated without turning session history into memory or forcing promotion.

entries.name: owner-layer-triage
entries.summary: Route one bounded reusable unit to one primary owner layer and one rejected nearest-wrong target so practice, workflow, scenario, proof, recall, and role surfaces stay distinct instead of collapsing into generic reuse.

entries.name: harvest-packet-contract
entries.summary: Keep one bounded HARVEST_PACKET contract over a reviewed session so downstream routing, diagnosis, repair, progression, and quest seams can consume explicit packet fields without silently replacing one another.

entries.name: decision-fork-cards
entries.summary: Turn one reviewed session or harvest packet into explicit decision fork cards so materially different next routes stay visible with gains, costs, owner targets, and stop conditions instead of collapsing into one hidden recommendation.

entries.name: risk-passport-lift
entries.summary: Attach one small risk passport to each explicit next route so difficulty, risk, control mode, delegate tier, and stop-condition posture stay visible without turning branch analysis into hidden routing policy.

entries.name: session-drift-taxonomy
entries.summary: Classify repeated post-session friction into bounded drift types so diagnosis can say what kind of problem is present before it claims one probable cause, owner hint, or repair shape.

entries.name: diagnosis-from-reviewed-evidence
entries.summary: Turn reviewed friction evidence into a bounded diagnosis packet that separates symptoms from probable causes, preserves unknowns, and names likely owner hints without mutating anything yet.

entries.name: repair-shape-from-diagnosis
entries.summary: Turn a reviewed diagnosis packet into the smallest honest repair shape so the next artifact stays bounded, owner-aware, and smaller than a scenario rollout.

entries.name: checkpoint-bound-self-repair
entries.summary: Keep self-repair behind explicit checkpoint posture with approval, rollback, health checks, iteration limits, and improvement-log visibility so repair stays reviewable instead of feeling like silent self-modification.

entries.name: progression-evidence-lift
entries.summary: Lift reviewed session evidence into a bounded multi-axis progression delta with explicit verdicts and small unlock hints so growth stays descriptive and evidence-backed instead of collapsing into one score.

entries.name: multi-axis-quest-overlay
entries.summary: Add quest-, RPG-, or chronicle-shaped reflection to a bounded multi-axis progression result so route legibility improves without letting flavor overwrite owner truth, proof, or routing authority.

entries.name: automation-fit-matrix
entries.summary: Classify one recurring manual route across repeat signal, determinism, proof posture, reversibility, and approval sensitivity so automation desire becomes a bounded verdict rather than vague enthusiasm.

entries.name: human-loop-to-first-landing
entries.summary: Route one recurring human loop to the first honest automation-facing landing so source-route-ready candidates become bounded skills or playbook source-route while unstable routes stay manual, repair-bound, or deferred.

entries.name: approval-sensitivity-check
entries.summary: Classify whether an automation candidate crosses approval, rollback, or self-change boundaries so checkpoint-required posture appears before any source-route-ready claim becomes credible.

entries.name: quest-unit-promotion-review
entries.summary: Review one repeated reviewed quest unit and emit one bounded promotion verdict so leaf workflow, route, role, proof, and recall surfaces do not collapse into generic reuse pressure.

entries.name: nearest-wrong-target-rejection
entries.summary: Reject the nearest wrong promotion target explicitly so repeated reviewed work does not collapse into the most convenient adjacent owner layer.

entries.name: workspace-root-ingress-and-mutation-gate
entries.summary: Enter federated workspaces through one explicit ingress pass and gate risky mutation through one explicit guard pass so session posture stays reviewable instead of hiding in operator memory.

entries.name: audit-to-closeout-proof-loop
entries.summary: Turn a reviewed audit finding set into a live-confirmed, proof-backed closeout loop so remediation claims rest on named evidence instead of audit wording alone.

entries.name: recommendation-truth-vs-host-actionability
entries.summary: Keep router recommendation truth separate from host actionability so non-executable recommendations stay visible, canonical install roots stay authoritative, and runnable actions do not masquerade as merely relevant advice.

entries.name: canonical-owner-with-validated-mirror
entries.summary: Keep one canonical cross-repo contract owner and allow local mirrors only when explicit parity validation keeps owner metadata and vocabulary exactly aligned.

entries.name: github-only-owner-endcap-with-reality-sync
entries.summary: Close a remote-only owner surface through GitHub-native issue and PR flow, then rebind staging and reality checks to the merged owner anchors so source-route-garden truth does not outlive the landing.

entries.name: pinned-validation-matrix-before-generated-publish
entries.summary: Rebuild generated outputs against the same workflow-pinned sibling refs that CI will validate before publish so local green does not overstate merge-readiness.

entries.name: degrade-reground-recover
entries.summary: Continue safely in a bounded degraded mode, reground against stronger sources, and recover later through explicit source-owned evidence instead of hidden repair theater.

entries.name: receipt-first-failure-analysis
entries.summary: Start failure review from source-owned receipts, separate facts from hypotheses, and tie any recovery change to explicit evidence rather than folklore or dashboard mythology.

entries.name: isolated-service-stop-on-shared-substrate
entries.summary: Stop one bounded service while keeping shared substrate services alive, then verify both target absence and substrate continuity so closeout does not widen into unnecessary teardown.

entries.name: stress-receipt-reground-closeout
entries.summary: Record one bounded stress event, preserve the smallest honest continuation, route through owner layers, and close out with reviewed evidence before any later proof reading.

entries.name: local-pattern-adoption-gate
entries.summary: Gate one shared pattern before local adoption by requiring owner consent, compatibility evidence, rollback, and retention watch so precedent does not silently become durable behavior.

entries.name: skill-proposal-handoff-packet
entries.summary: Emit one bounded skill-proposal handoff packet from a technique-side adoption review so reusable practice does not become skill acceptance or activation by implication.

entries.name: adopted-practice-retention-review
entries.summary: Review one adopted practice against current evidence, usefulness, drift, and rollback posture so it stays active only while retention remains explicit.

entries.name: superseded-practice-obsolescence-route
entries.summary: Route one adopted practice toward supersession, merge, reanchor, defer, drop, or deprecation review with owner receipt, retained lesson, and provenance intact.

entries.name: single-missing-evidence-request
entries.summary: Ask for exactly one missing evidence object that could change a bounded review state so review can narrow without broad research, verdict overclaim, or proof theater.

entries.name: single-scoped-evidence-reference
entries.summary: Offer exactly one scoped evidence reference with relevance, support limit, and reliance condition so review can use a source without treating it as proof, verdict, or source-truth transfer.

entries.name: single-locus-claim-challenge
entries.summary: Challenge exactly one claim at one vulnerable locus, naming pressure reason and next support question so review can apply pressure without turning challenge into proof, tone, or adjudication.

entries.name: aoa-checkpoint-closeout-bridge
entries.summary: Carry provisional checkpoint hints into an explicit reviewed closeout sequence without treating notes or mechanical reports as verdict authority. Use to collect bounded checkpoint hints or to execute the reviewed harvest, progression, quest-promotion, and branch stages after a session artifact is closed and reviewed. Do not use mid-session for final judgments, without reviewed evidence, as a hidden playbook runner, or to write owner truth.

entries.name: aoa-decision
entries.summary: Find, record, or correct durable repository decisions through one decision family. Use when prior rationale, a meaningful accepted choice, supersession, or drift between an authored decision record and its derived decision indexes is the task. Select exactly one internal mode and keep authored owner records authoritative. Do not use for ordinary docs edits, current source-of-truth conflicts, or generated/runtime authority mapping.

entries.name: aoa-engineering-shape
entries.summary: An implicit match may suggest this skill, but must not load or execute it until explicit invocation or a source-authorized parent-route selection. Shape one software responsibility boundary through bounded-context, core-boundary, or port-adapter mode. Use when responsibilities, reusable rules, orchestration, or concrete dependencies are entangled and a small owner-aware boundary is needed before implementation. Do not use for source-authority lookup, ordinary edits, test design, or a boundary that is already clear.

entries.name: aoa-eval
entries.summary: Select or apply an eval, route a confirmed no-fit into owner-local intake or design, or classify supplied or retrieved session hits into provenance-bound eval candidates. Use for one cross-repository or owner-local evaluation need; compose with session-memory for retrieval and with aoa-verification after apply. Do not use for named central proof bundles, results, reports, verdicts, or lifecycle work (`aoa-evals`), ordinary already-named tests, undefined invariants, or generic session harvesting.

entries.name: aoa-knowledge-stewardship
entries.summary: Sanitize private technical material into a public-safe owner-bounded derivative, or resolve authority among authored, generated, runtime, and installed sources. Use when incidents, logs, configs, diagnostics, credentials, private paths, host/network topology, or conflicting sources require a named-audience review draft or authority map. Preserve raw custody and separate artifact creation from publication. Do not use for memo writeback, session mining, direct publication execution, durable-memory authority, or ordinary prose editing.

entries.name: aoa-memo-writeback
entries.summary: PATH GATE: copy the host-appended `(file: .../SKILL.md)` locator verbatim for the first read; never construct `.system` or `$CODEX_HOME` paths. If one constructed path has already failed with not-found before any other action, recover exactly once by reading the host locator and report `package_path_recovered`; never search or probe again. FIRST-WRITEBACK ONLY: use before any memo artifact exists to turn live-session, closeout, landed-work, PR, diff, or review evidence into one guarded candidate/export/no-write decision. If an existing candidate, export, quarantine packet, memory object, corpus identity, lifecycle target, or read-model target is named or supplied, select aoa-memo directly. Do not use for eval mining, raw session maintenance, generic harvest, durable memory, owner intake/review, recall, or proof.

entries.name: aoa-session-harvest
entries.summary: EXPLICIT REVIEWED-SESSION INPUT ONLY: turn a named closed/reviewed session packet into extract, classify, promote, branch, or automation-opportunity output. A natural work closeout asking whether anything reusable should be preserved for the owner's future work belongs to aoa-memo-writeback, not this skill. Use on live/unreviewed session material only when extraction or promotion is explicitly requested and the required result is blocked_unreviewed_evidence. Do not use for generic summarization, progression, memo/writeback judgment, direct promotion, scheduling, mutation, owner writes, or telemetry authority.

entries.name: aoa-session-recovery
entries.summary: Diagnose reviewed recurring session or workflow failure, or carry one bounded owner repair from diagnosis through checkpoint, execution posture, rollback, and real health verification. Use when evidence shows contamination, drift, repeated route/tool failure, or a reviewed diagnosis is ready for repair. Do not use on live/unreviewed material, for vague self-improvement, or to claim a proposed or merely executed change is verified.

entries.name: aoa-verification
entries.summary: An implicit match may suggest this skill, but must not load or execute it until explicit invocation or a source-authorized parent-route selection. Turn one stable technical truth into bounded evidence through contract, coverage-audit, or property mode. Use to define a named consumer seam, audit what existing checks really constrain, or express a broad invariant after manual cases establish meaning. Compose with aoa-eval when an evaluation surface must first be found or applied. Do not use for undefined behavior, generic test plans, or green checks as whole-system proof.

entries.name: aoa-a2a-summon-return-checkpoint
entries.summary: Checks whether a reviewed summon child route preserves the contract from aoa-summon request through sdk.a2a decision, Codex-local target, reviewed child result, checkpoint bridge, memo writeback, and runtime dry-run receipt.

entries.name: aoa-ambiguity-handling
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent handles incomplete, conflicting, or underspecified task meaning on bounded change tasks without silently choosing an unearned path.

entries.name: aoa-antifragility-posture
entries.summary: Checks whether one owner-local surface handles a named stressor family through bounded degraded continuation, source-owned receipts, and split-axis readout without stealing source ownership.

entries.name: aoa-approval-boundary-adherence
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent correctly distinguishes safe action, explicit-approval-required action, and out-of-bounds action on bounded task surfaces.

entries.name: aoa-artifact-review-rubric
entries.summary: Checks whether a produced artifact on a bounded change task is reviewably strong on the visible task surface without treating polish as proof of workflow discipline.

entries.name: aoa-bounded-change-quality
entries.summary: Checks whether a non-trivial agent change stays scoped, explicitly verified, and clearly reported without silent task expansion.

entries.name: aoa-candidate-lineage-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether a growth-refinery lineage chain stays internally coherent across checkpoint carry, reviewed candidate, source-route staging, and downstream owner evidence without inferring stronger landing than the artifacts support.

entries.name: aoa-compost-provenance-preservation
entries.summary: Checks whether witness-derived compost artifacts preserve provenance, review state, limits, and contradiction posture as they move from raw input toward note, principle, or canon-candidate surfaces.

entries.name: aoa-continuity-anchor-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether a bounded self-agency continuity window keeps an inspectable anchor chain across continuity, revision, reanchor, and anchor refs without letting memo, stats, or chat residue become continuity truth.

entries.name: aoa-diagnosis-cause-discipline
entries.summary: Checks whether a diagnosis or self-diagnosis move names causal hypotheses, evidence limits, and unknowns without confusing symptoms, owner ambiguity, or repair success with cause proof.

entries.name: aoa-eval-integrity-check
entries.summary: Checks whether current public starter bundles stay coherent across manifest contract, verdict wording, evidence coverage, and public selection surfaces, and serves as the bounded integrity sidecar for comparison-spine wording passes.

entries.name: aoa-experience-certification-gate-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether an experience-derived release candidate has bounded evidence for authorized operator certification review without letting the eval certify.

entries.name: aoa-experience-protocol-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether an experience verdict bundle keeps protocol integrity explicit, bounded, and weaker than runtime or governance authority.

entries.name: aoa-local-text-contract-fit
entries.summary: Checks whether a local text lane can satisfy a compact four-case contract pack without upgrading bounded control prompts into broad capability claims.

entries.name: aoa-long-horizon-depth
entries.summary: Checks whether a checkpoint-based long-horizon inquiry can relaunch with its axis, contradictions, and canon-vs-memory boundaries still legible.

entries.name: aoa-longitudinal-growth-snapshot
entries.summary: Checks whether ordered, comparable windows on the same bounded workflow surface show modest directional movement without upgrading that movement into broad capability growth.

entries.name: aoa-memo-contradiction-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether lifecycle-aware memo consumers keep superseded, retracted, and still-open contradiction posture explicit instead of flattening memory tension into one smooth current story.

entries.name: aoa-memo-recall-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether memo recall stays narrow, provenance-visible, and staleness-honest across the first inspect/capsule/expand consumer contour.

entries.name: aoa-memo-reviewed-candidate-adoption-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether reviewed distillation candidates can reach adopted memo visibility, live receipt visibility, and recall surfaces without flattening candidate posture into settled truth.

entries.name: aoa-memo-write-path-guardrails
entries.summary: Checks whether aoa-memo write-path candidates preserve source trust, ingestion risk, derivation lineage, action-safety separation, and reviewed landing gates before durable memory.

entries.name: aoa-memo-writeback-act-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether one bounded runtime-to-memo writeback act stays review-gated, receipt-visible, and recall-aligned from runtime closure into adopted memo surfaces.

entries.name: aoa-memo-writeback-decision-quality
entries.summary: Checks whether an aoa-memo-writeback application made a bounded, evidence-linked memory writeback decision with adequate route choice, source and session search, owner-boundary clarity, noise rejection, privacy limits, and missed-evidence disclosure.

entries.name: aoa-output-vs-process-gap
entries.summary: Compares artifact-side and process-side readings on the same bounded cases to show when polished output outruns workflow discipline, process outruns artifact strength, or the two stay aligned.

entries.name: aoa-owner-fit-routing-quality
entries.summary: Checks whether a reviewed growth-refinery candidate is routed to the right owner layer with one clear owner hypothesis, honest nearest-wrong-target reasoning, and no first-authoring drift into derivative repos.

entries.name: aoa-recurrence-control-plane-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether recurrence control-plane artifacts preserve typed propagation, observation-only hooks, owner review boundaries, thin downstream projections, and Agon stop-lines.

entries.name: aoa-reflective-revision-boundedness
entries.summary: Checks whether reflective revision stays inside one named revision window, keeps stop-lines visible, and does not widen into vague continuity or hidden autonomy.

entries.name: aoa-regression-same-task
entries.summary: Compares a candidate against a frozen baseline on the same bounded task family to detect material regression without claiming general growth.

entries.name: aoa-repair-boundedness
entries.summary: Checks whether a reanchor or self-repair move stays bounded, preserves owner boundaries, and leaves a reviewable trail instead of smearing scope inflation across layers.

entries.name: aoa-return-anchor-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether a return-capable route names a real anchor and re-enters or stops without faking continuity.

entries.name: aoa-ring-application-discipline
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent applies a named skill ring through an explicit applicability map, honest defer-skip decisions, and a visible harvest check instead of ceremonial blanket execution.

entries.name: aoa-runtime-latency-tradeoff
entries.summary: Checks whether a runtime comparison preserves a bounded latency and resource-use tradeoff claim without overreading it as reasoning quality, agent behavior, or cross-host performance.

entries.name: aoa-scope-drift-detection
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent keeps requested scope aligned with executed scope on bounded change tasks, or explicitly discloses deviation.

entries.name: aoa-self-reanchor-correctness
entries.summary: Checks whether a bounded self-agency continuity route reanchors to the last valid artifact, keeps return mode explicit, and refuses chat-residue continuity when anchor integrity is lost.

entries.name: aoa-stats-regrounding-boundary-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether stats-derived re-grounding signals preserve the split between stats observability, SDK policy, routing advisory hints, and bounded eval proof verdicts.

entries.name: aoa-stress-recovery-window
entries.summary: Checks whether a named stressor family shows healthier handling across an ordered bounded window without letting proof, stats, routing, or memo outrank owner evidence.

entries.name: aoa-tool-trajectory-discipline
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent uses tools in a disciplined, reviewable way on bounded tasks where the tool path itself is part of the bounded claim.

entries.name: aoa-trace-outcome-separation
entries.summary: Checks whether bounded change workflows remain reviewable when final outcome and execution-path quality are judged separately before any combined reading.

entries.name: aoa-verification-honesty
entries.summary: Checks whether an agent truthfully reports which verification steps on a bounded change task were executed, skipped, or blocked.

entries.name: aoa-witness-trace-integrity
entries.summary: Checks whether a public witness trace for a bounded run keeps meaningful steps, tool visibility, state deltas, failures, redaction posture, and the markdown summary aligned enough for review.

entries.name: charter
entries.summary: Role, mission, and hard rules of aoa-memo as the explicit memory and recall layer.

entries.name: memory-model
entries.summary: Conceptual model for functions, temperatures, scopes, trust posture, and KAG orientation.

entries.name: boundaries
entries.summary: Ownership map for what belongs in aoa-memo and what should stay in neighboring AoA or ToS layers.

entries.name: roadmap
entries.summary: Milestones for turning aoa-memo from bootstrap doctrine into a routeable, provenance-aware memory layer.

entries.name: narrative-core-contract
entries.summary: Contract for keeping authored/core memory distinct from derived memory surfaces and downstream substrate work.

entries.name: runtime-writeback-seam
entries.summary: Bounded contract for mapping runtime-local state, checkpoint exports, and reviewed distillation candidates into the current memo canon.

entries.name: agent-memory-posture-seam
entries.summary: Memo-side contract note for the posture fields that aoa-agents may consume without moving role policy into aoa-memo.

entries.name: playbook-memory-scopes
entries.summary: Playbook-facing guidance for bounded memory scopes, recall modes, and checkpoint-oriented memo use.

entries.name: kag-tos-bridge-contract
entries.summary: Contract for chunk-face and graph-face bridge exports that preserve ToS references and keep KAG lifts downstream.

entries.name: memory-eval-guardrails
entries.summary: Memo-side guardrail handoff surface that keeps recall, provenance, staleness, leakage, and merge risks explicit for aoa-evals.

entries.name: operational-boundary
entries.summary: V1-facing boundary note that fixes aoa-memo as doctrine plus compact public surfaces rather than runtime memory infrastructure.

entries.name: routing-memory-adoption
entries.summary: Router-first adoption note that formalizes additive inspect, capsule, and expand consumption over doctrine and curated object memo surfaces.

entries.name: operational-gate
entries.summary: Operational memory admission mechanic for deployment incidents, office/service incidents, service revision ledgers, and post-release boundaries.

entries.name: recurrence-support
entries.summary: Route-return support mechanic for bounded relaunch anchors, witness trace exports, and reviewed closeout recall landings.

entries.name: lineage-harvest
entries.summary: Pattern-lineage memory mechanic for cross-repo recurring signals, harvest gates, and downstream dossier boundaries.
